title: Progressive baseline

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Progressive baseline; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Progressive baseline
sources.claim: Progressive enhancement begins with meaningful HTML and core actions, then adds CSS and JavaScript without removing baseline content or navigation.

sources.locator: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2; W3C Recommendation 2023; provisions applicable to Progressive baseline
sources.claim: Progressive enhancement begins with meaningful HTML and core actions, then adds CSS and JavaScript without removing baseline content or navigation.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Progressive enhancement begins with meaningful HTML and core actions, then adds CSS and JavaScript without removing baseline content or navigation.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A server-rendered course list remains readable and linked before JavaScript adds filtering and saved progress. Your goal is to demonstrate the site with styles and scripts unavailable and document acceptable degradation. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Metadata and accessibility audit; Images and media; Data tables; Forms and native controls; Semantic content review; Meaningful links. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Progressive enhancement begins with meaningful HTML and core actions, then adds CSS and JavaScript without removing baseline content or navigation. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Some applications require scripting, but loading, failure, and unsupported states still need intentional communication and recovery. This draft therefore rejects: Progressive enhancement means every advanced interaction must work identically with JavaScript disabled.

includes: Ensure core content and navigation remain understandable before CSS and JavaScript load.
Demonstrate the site with styles and scripts unavailable and document acceptable degradation.
excludes: Some applications require scripting, but loading, failure, and unsupported states still need intentional communication and recovery.

outcomes: Demonstrate the site with styles and scripts unavailable and document acceptable degradation.

examples.title: Progressive baseline: concrete case
examples.situation: A server-rendered course list remains readable and linked before JavaScript adds filtering and saved progress.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A server-rendered course list remains readable and linked before JavaScript adds filtering and saved progress.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Start from meaningful resilient HTML and define honest fallbacks or failure states for enhancements that require scripting.
examples.result: The result follows because Start from meaningful resilient HTML and define honest fallbacks or failure states for enhancements that require scripting.

examples.title: Progressive baseline: boundary case
examples.situation: Some applications require scripting, but loading, failure, and unsupported states still need intentional communication and recovery.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Some applications require scripting, but loading, failure, and unsupported states still need intentional communication and recovery.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Progressive enhancement means every advanced interaction must work identically with JavaScript disabled.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Start from meaningful resilient HTML and define honest fallbacks or failure states for enhancements that require scripting.

analogy.example: A labeled document outline can model Progressive baseline, with structure carrying meaning before decoration.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Progressive baseline before using exact platform terms
limitations: Documents cannot model parser recovery, browser APIs, or interactive semantics exactly.
